scope: Cross-stage synthesis of the same-night GPT-5.5 LexBench stability campaign. Stage 4 adds no new Agent trajectory and does not pool unlike task sets into a new quality estimate.

stage1_population_sample.question: Does either browser backend retain a general quality lead after counterbalanced repeats?

stage2_mechanism_holdout.question: Are the selected site-level directional differences reproducible, and what failure evidence accompanies them?
stage2_mechanism_holdout.answer: Directional effects were mostly reproducible but bidirectional and site-specific; the diagnostic set is selection-biased.

stage3_capacity_resources.question: Is quota 64 attainable, and what is the highest complete formal concurrency observed tonight?
stage3_capacity_resources.answer: Quota 64 was directly attained once with balanced allocation; c32 is the highest complete and observed formal point.

execution_ledger.reason: The 12 arms mix a 64-task sample, a selected 16-task diagnostic holdout, and different concurrency treatments.

supported_claims.claim: The campaign did not establish a general Lexmount or Local quality winner on the fixed 64-task sample.
supported_claims.evidence: Stage 1 repeated difference +1.5625pp, 95% cluster CI [-9.375, +12.5]pp; Stage 3 c32 was 40/64 versus 41/64.

supported_claims.claim: Some site-level backend differences are reproducible and bidirectional.
supported_claims.confidence: medium-high for the selected diagnostic tasks
supported_claims.evidence: Stage 2 confirmed 16/18 expected directional holdout observations; four tasks were Lexmount-only and three Local-only in all four repeats.

supported_claims.claim: Lexmount removes local Chrome resource load from the runner and materially lowers runner-scope CPU and memory.
supported_claims.confidence: high for runner scope, unknown end-to-end
supported_claims.evidence: At c32, Local used 4.046x CPU, 2.086x mean PSS, and 2.476x kernel peak while delivering 1.214x rollout throughput.

supported_claims.claim: The account can hold 64 active Lexmount sessions, but the task-shaped 28/36 allocation is not yet reliable at that high-water mark.
supported_claims.confidence: high for attainability, medium for reliability boundary
supported_claims.evidence: Balanced 32/32 reached 64 for 35 samples; the task-shaped split failed twice.

unsupported_claims: Lexmount is generally more accurate than Local Chrome on LexBench.
The observed access differences are proven to be caused by IP, proxy, browser fingerprint, or any single platform mechanism.
A quota of 64 guarantees that any profile mix can obtain 64 simultaneous sessions.
Lexmount controller memory is the end-to-end resource cost of the remote browser service.
One c16 and one c32 run prove that concurrency causes a quality increase or decrease.

operational_default.admission_preflight: Use the actual EN/ZH allocation before any higher-concurrency formal run.
operational_default.lexmount_sustainability_gate: Require the session monitor to observe the named target, zero monitor errors, and zero residual sessions.
operational_default.c64_gate: Require two consecutive successful 28 EN + 36 ZH preflights, or provider-side reservation/allocation evidence, before a formal c64 run.
operational_default.routing: Treat the stable site-level directions as fallback/routing candidates and validate them on fresh holdouts before productizing.

next_experiments.experiment: Repeat counterbalanced c32 pairs in at least two additional time windows.
next_experiments.decision_changed: Whether c32 quality remains near parity and whether throughput/resource ratios persist.

next_experiments.experiment: Repeat task-shaped 28 EN + 36 ZH admission until the c64 gate is passed or provider allocation evidence identifies the constraint.
next_experiments.decision_changed: Whether a formal c64 arm is honest and reproducible.

next_experiments.experiment: Build a fresh, unselected site-routing holdout for the access-sensitive and control-sensitive signatures.
next_experiments.decision_changed: Whether backend-aware fallback improves population performance without selection bias.
